medications: Naproxen 500 mg as needed for pain (limited use due to blood pressure)
Cyclobenzaprine 5 mg at night as needed for muscle spasms
Melatonin 3 mg as needed for sleep
health.mental_health: High stress related to potential eviction and irregular income; reports anxiety spikes, trouble sleeping, and occasional panic-like symptoms when dealing with landlord notices. Avoids discussing it with most clients and friends.
recent_visits: Primary care visit at Denver Health (BP follow-up; medication adjustment) - 6 weeks ago
Physical therapy evaluation for lumbar pain (referred by PCP) - 3 months ago
Urgent care visit for severe back flare after moving boxes (received muscle relaxant) - 2 months ago

finance.debt: About $9,800 in credit card debt from covering rent during slow client months; behind on current rent by roughly one month and negotiating a payment plan to avoid eviction. Also has a small medical balance (~$650) from imaging/PT visits.
recent_transactions: Partial rent payment to property management (short payment) - last week
Late fee payment and notice processing fee - last month
Car insurance premium (paid in installments) - this month
Payment to Xcel Energy to prevent shutoff - 3 weeks ago

relationships: Kenji Tanaka (older brother in Seattle; supportive but they argue about asking family for money)
Marisol Ortega (close friend in Denver; occasionally lets Priya store boxes and use her address for deliveries)
Jordan Lee (former partner; limited contact, tension over an old shared lease debt)

social.social_media_activity: Maintains a polished Instagram and Behance presence for design work; posts sporadically and avoids personal content. Uses Facebook groups for Denver freelance leads and a local mutual-aid group under limited visibility settings.
private_conversations: Texting Marisol about needing a temporary place to sleep if the eviction goes through
Messaging a mutual-aid group about emergency rental assistance and food pantry hours
Email thread with landlord negotiating a payment plan and asking to delay filing
Phone calls with brother about whether she can borrow money without disclosing full details to extended family

schedule.work_hours: Typically 10:00am–4:00pm weekdays, with evening sprints (8:00pm–11:00pm) near deadlines; hours fluctuate based on client load and health flare-ups.
regular_appointments: Blood pressure check-in (telehealth or pharmacy kiosk) every 2–3 weeks
Physical therapy home exercise routine (20 minutes, 4x/week)
Monthly check-in call with a housing counselor through a Denver nonprofit
upcoming_events: Court date/mediation window for eviction filing (tentative; awaiting confirmation) - within 2 weeks
Client brand presentation call for a local brewery rebrand - next Tuesday
Community resource appointment for rental assistance application submission - Friday morning
schedule.daily_routine: Starts with coffee and checking client emails, then design work in blocks with frequent stretch breaks for back pain. Midday errands often include pharmacy or printing. Evenings are used for invoicing, job outreach, and packing/organizing in case she has to move quickly.

professional.company: Independent contractor working with Denver-area small businesses and a few remote marketing agencies (operates under the name 'Tanaka Studio')
projects: Logo and packaging refresh for a LoDo coffee roaster
Social media ad templates for a Denver fitness studio (retainer, month-to-month)
Pitch deck and one-sheet design for a local nonprofit housing fundraiser
Website hero graphics and UI assets for a Boulder-based SaaS startup (remote)
professional.performance_issues: Occasional missed morning meetings and slower turnaround during back pain flares; has had to request one deadline extension recently due to an urgent housing appointment and stress-related insomnia. Worried that instability (quiet workspace, reliable internet) could impact client confidence.
workplace_relationships: Primary agency contact: Erin Caldwell (project manager at a small marketing agency; generally supportive but strict about deadlines)
Print vendor relationship: Mile High Print Co. (good rapport; sometimes negotiates partial payments)
Client tension: owner of the fitness studio questioned availability after a rescheduled call

hobbies: Urban sketching at parks and cafes
Hiking easy trails (Cherry Creek State Park when back allows)
Listening to design podcasts and audiobooks from the library
habits: Uses a heating pad and stretches most evenings for back pain
Tracks blood pressure at home when stressed
Keeps a 'go bag' and important documents organized due to fear of sudden move-out
Limits social outings to save money; often chooses free events or library spaces
Occasional late-night work sessions fueled by caffeine to meet deadlines
lifestyle.living_situation: Rents a small older one-bedroom apartment in the West Colfax area of Denver; behind on rent and has received a pay-or-quit notice. Has begun packing non-essentials and has arranged with a friend to store a few boxes if she needs to relocate quickly. Tries to keep the situation hidden from most clients.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Tries to follow a lower-sodium diet for hypertension (more home-cooked meals when possible), but relies on inexpensive convenience foods during high-stress weeks; limits alcohol and avoids heavy meals at night to help sleep.
